perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information ” shall mean scientific, research, technical or business information pertaining to the Field , including but not limited to inventions ; know -how ; trade secrets ; techniques ; processes ; designs ; drawings ; product designs ; formulae and analysis ; and any business information, including but not limited to price lists ; customer lists ; cost analyses ; reports ; surveys , market information and data . In respect of Confidential Information disclosed in documentary form, model or any other tangible form, this shall be marked by the Disclosing Party as confidential or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implicatio n that it is imparted in confidence. Any Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives orally or visually, shall be identified as such prior to disclosure and summarized in a writing provided by the Disclosing Party to t he Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of the date of initial disclosure. In 2/5 case of disagreement regarding said summary, the Receiving Party shall make any objections in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of the written summary.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information ” shall mean scientific, research, technical or business information pertaining to the Field , including but not limited to inventions ; know -how ; techniques ; processes ; designs ; drawings ; product designs ; formulae and analysis ; and any business information, including but not limited to price lists ; customer lists ; cost analyses ; reports ; surveys , market information and data . In respect of Confidential Information disclosed in documentary form, model or any other tangible form, this shall be marked by the Disclosing Party as confidential or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implicatio n that it is imparted in confidence. Any Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives orally or visually, shall be identified as such prior to disclosure and summarized in a writing provided by the Disclosing Party to t he Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of the date of initial disclosure. In 2/5 case of disagreement regarding said summary, the Receiving Party shall make any objections in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of the written summary.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'trade secrets' from the definition of 'Confidential Information', Section 1 now contradicts Section 3(a), which obligates the Receiving Parties 'To take proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of the Confidential Information'. If 'trade secrets' are no longer considered 'Confidential Information', the obligation to protect them is undermined, causing uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of confidentiality and non -use The Receiving Parties undertake and agree : a) To take proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of the Confidential Information ; b) Not to disclose , or allow it to be disclosed , in whole or in part to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party c) Not to use , in whole or in part, the Confidential Information for anything other tha n the Permitted Purpose. d) Not to use the Confidential Information for their own commercial use or for that of any other person or party , without prior written consent by the Disclosing Party ; e) Not to make copies of the Confidential Information in any format without prior written authorisation by the Disclosing Party ; f) Not to alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any Confi dential Information; g) Not to apply for any patent or trademark which could include any Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'trade secrets' in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The changed text removes 'trade secrets' from this definition. Section 3(a), the contradicted text, obligates the Receiving Parties 'To take proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of the Confidential Information'. By removing 'trade secrets' from the definition, the obligation to protect trade secrets is undermined, creating a direct conflict and contradiction. The change therefore breaks compatibility between the definition of Confidential Information and the obligation to protect it.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is and remains the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is transferred and becomes the property of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the sentence from 'remains the property of the Disclosing Party' to 'becomes the property of the Receiving Party' directly contradicts the intent and language of clause 5, particularly the first sentence and the rest of section 5 as a whole. It creates immediate uncertainty over who owns what and when.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of information and property The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is transferred and becomes the property of the Receiving Party. The Receiving party must, at the end of this Agreement or within seven days following written instruction by the Disclosing Party to do so, return or destroy all Confidential Information in the Receiving Party 's possession, custody or control. The Receiving Party may retain one (1) copy of any such Confidential Information to be stored securely with its legal representative only if required for regulatory or insura nce purposes , but in this event the confidentiality and non -use provisions in t his Agreement remain in place with respect to such retained Confidential I nformation. The return or destruction of any Confidential Information will not diminish or otherwise affect any other obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the 'Confidential Information is transferred and becomes the property of the Receiving Party.' The contradicted text, which now contains the same sentence due to the change, goes on to say 'The Receiving party must...return or destroy all Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction because the Receiving Party cannot be both the owner of the information and obligated to return or destroy it. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because the clause is repeated with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no contractual obligation to any Party to enter into any separate business collaboration for the development of the Field or associated technology . The Disclosing Party is not obliged to disclose any Confidential Information to the Receiving Party and the Receiving Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any information prior to any disclosure .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes a contractual obligation on the Disclosing Party to enter into a separate business collaboration for the development of the Field or associated technology . The Disclosing Party is obliged to disclose all Confidential Information to the Receiving Party and the Receiving Party shall not have the right to refuse to accept any information prior to any disclosure .
perturbation.explanation: Changing the terms of this section now creates a contradiction. The original clause stated no obligation to enter into business together. The change now places a full obligation to collaborate. In addition, the Disclosing Party is now obliged to disclose *all* Confidential Information, while the receiving party *cannot* refuse any.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No obligation This Agreement imposes a contractual obligation on the Disclosing Party to enter into a separate business collaboration for the development of the Field or associated technology . The Disclosing Party is obliged to disclose all Confidential Information to the Receiving Party and the Receiving Party shall not have the right to refuse to accept any information prior to any disclosure .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that there was no obligation to enter into a business collaboration or disclose confidential information, and the receiving party could refuse information. The changed text creates a direct contradiction by imposing a contractual obligation for collaboration, requiring the disclosure of all confidential information, and preventing the receiving party from refusing any information. This directly conflicts with the 'No obligation' contradicted text which restates the new obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice (registered letter with proof of receipt) to the other Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding this, no Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The inserted text, 'no Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement under any circumstances,' creates a direct in-text contradiction within Section 10. The first part says you can terminate with 30 days' notice, and the second part states this is impossible and the section contradicts itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, no Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement under any circumstances. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states 'no Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement under any circumstances'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date'. The changed text makes it impossible for the agreement to be terminated while the contradicted text outlines a specific termination date, and so, there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the ordinary Courts of Trieste .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the International Chamber of Commerce in Paris, France.
perturbation.explanation: The section originally placed jurisdiction in the 'ordinary Courts of Trieste.' The change now contradicts this, stating jurisdiction is now within the International Chamber of Commerce in Paris, France, This will lead to legal disputes over which court has proper jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Applicable Law an d Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the International Chamber of Commerce in Paris, France.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the 'ordinary Courts of Trieste' for dispute resolution, which is directly contradicted by the changed text that specifies the 'International Chamber of Commerce in Paris, France'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning it now also states that the jurisdiction is the International Chamber of Commerce in Paris, France. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict as to where legal disputes should be handled, creating a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments shall be binding on their successors in title and assigns , and undertake the necessary steps to ensure they are so bound.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments shall not be binding on their successors in title and assigns , and undertake the necessary steps to ensure they are not so bound.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause binds successors in title and assigns; the new clause explicitly prevents this. Thus is now a direct contradiction and affects the entire agreement's long-term validity and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Successor and assigns The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments shall not be binding on their successors in title and assigns , and undertake the necessary steps to ensure they are not so bound.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is binding on successors and assigns, while the changed text explicitly states it is not. The contradicted text repeats the changed text 'The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments shall not be binding on their successors in title and assigns , and undertake the necessary steps to ensure they are not so bound.'. Thus, the changed text creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. This fits the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as it places a clause in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
